name: CKM mixing pattern
summary: The CKM matrix elements organise as powers of a single small parameter lambda ~ 0.225 (Cabibbo angle). |V_us| ~ lambda, |V_cb| ~ A*lambda^2, |V_ub| ~ A*lambda^3*(rho - i*eta). This is the Wolfenstein parameterization. The pattern is so striking that it suggests a hidden expansion parameter, but the SM does not derive it.

characteristic_numbers.note: The Cabibbo angle. Diagonal elements ~1 - lambda^2/2 are O(1); first-off-diagonal are O(lambda); 1-3 mixing is O(lambda^3).
Roughly lambda^2; tracking the geometric sequence rather than random.

principles_stressed.note: The numerical near-coincidence between the Wolfenstein lambda and the down/strange Yukawa ratio (m_d/m_s)^{1/2} ~ lambda hints at a common origin in a flavor symmetry.

history: Cabibbo angle (1963), Kobayashi-Maskawa extension to three generations (1973). Wolfenstein parameterization (1983) recognised the lambda hierarchy. Froggatt-Nielsen explains it via integer U(1) charges; modular flavor symmetry (since ~2017) gives an alternative derivation.

references.citation: Wolfenstein, 'Parametrization of the Kobayashi-Maskawa Matrix', PRL 51, 1945 (1983)
Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics' (2024) - CKM section
